req.title: Bill pay (boundary variant)
req.statement: A customer shall pay a registered biller between $1 and $50,000 with deterministic settlement date calculation based on biller cut-off. Boundary behaviour at exact min/max thresholds must be tested.

doc.reasoning: R-FIN-032 defines a bill-pay endpoint with a strict numeric range [$1, $50000]. Classical BVA drives four boundary probes (1, 2, 49999, 50000) plus out-of-range probes (0, 50001). EP partitions the valid interior (e.g. $500) and two invalid partitions (<$1, >$50000). A positive EP test confirms the happy-path contract (201 + settlement_date). Two BVA tests confirm exact min and max thresholds are accepted. Two NEG tests confirm 422 is returned for amounts just outside each boundary. A DT test confirms that biller cut-off time drives a deterministic next-business-day vs same-day settlement decision. Each test is self-contained via the `registered_biller` and `auth_headers` fixtures and produces idempotent results by using unique idempotency keys.

tests.name: test_billpay_valid_midrange_amount
preconditions: Biller is registered in the system
expected: Response body contains 'settlement_date' as a valid ISO-8601 date
trace: R-FIN-032: payment scheduled; settlement date returned
tests.executable:
import uuid
import pytest
from datetime import datetime

def test_billpay_valid_midrange_amount(api_client, registered_biller, auth_headers):
    payload = {
        "biller_id": registered_biller["id"],
        "amount": "500.00",
        "idempotency_key": str(uuid.uuid4()),
    }
    resp = api_client.post("/billpay", json=payload, headers=auth_headers)
    assert resp.status_code == 201
    body = resp.json()
    assert "payment_id" in body
    assert "settlement_date" in body
    datetime.fromisoformat(body["settlement_date"])  # validates ISO-8601
    assert body["status"] == "SCHEDULED"

tests.name: test_billpay_amount_below_minimum_returns_422
preconditions: Biller is registered in the system
expected: Response body contains error code referencing amount range violation
trace: R-FIN-032: 422 when amount out of range
tests.executable:
import uuid
import pytest

def test_billpay_amount_below_minimum_returns_422(api_client, registered_biller, auth_headers):
    payload = {
        "biller_id": registered_biller["id"],
        "amount": "0.00",
        "idempotency_key": str(uuid.uuid4()),
    }
    resp = api_client.post("/billpay", json=payload, headers=auth_headers)
    assert resp.status_code == 422
    body = resp.json()
    assert "error" in body or "detail" in body
    error_text = str(body.get("error", body.get("detail", ""))).lower()
    assert "amount" in error_text

tests.name: test_billpay_settlement_date_next_business_day_after_cutoff
preconditions: Biller is registered with a cut-off time of 17:00 local
Request is submitted after the biller cut-off time
actions: POST /billpay with amount=100.00, biller_id=<after_cutoff_biller_id>, submission_time=<after_cutoff_time>, idempotency_key=<uuid>
expected: settlement_date is the next business day after submission date
trace: R-FIN-032: deterministic settlement date calculation based on biller cut-off
DT: condition = after cut-off -> action = next business day
tests.executable:
import uuid
import pytest
from datetime import date, timedelta
from <helper_fn> import next_business_day

def test_billpay_settlement_date_next_business_day_after_cutoff(
    api_client, after_cutoff_biller, auth_headers, freeze_time_after_cutoff
):
    payload = {
        "biller_id": after_cutoff_biller["id"],
        "amount": "100.00",
        "idempotency_key": str(uuid.uuid4()),
    }
    resp = api_client.post("/billpay", json=payload, headers=auth_headers)
    assert resp.status_code == 201
    body = resp.json()
    assert body["status"] == "SCHEDULED"
    today = freeze_time_after_cutoff.date()
    expected_settlement = next_business_day(today + timedelta(days=1))
    assert body["settlement_date"] == expected_settlement.isoformat()

preconditions: Biller is registered with a cut-off time of 17:00 local
Request is submitted before the biller cut-off time on a business day

trace: R-FIN-032: deterministic settlement date calculation based on biller cut-off
DT: condition = before cut-off -> action = same-day settlement
tests.executable:
import uuid
import pytest

def test_billpay_settlement_date_same_day_before_cutoff(
    api_client, before_cutoff_biller, auth_headers, freeze_time_before_cutoff
):
    payload = {
        "biller_id": before_cutoff_biller["id"],
        "amount": "100.00",
        "idempotency_key": str(uuid.uuid4()),
    }
    resp = api_client.post("/billpay", json=payload, headers=auth_headers)
    assert resp.status_code == 201
    body = resp.json()
    assert body["status"] == "SCHEDULED"
    today = freeze_time_before_cutoff.date()
    assert body["settlement_date"] == today.isoformat()

tests.name: test_billpay_idempotency_duplicate_key_returns_same_payment
preconditions: Biller is registered in the system
First POST with idempotency_key K has already been accepted (SCHEDULED state)
actions: POST /billpay with amount=200.00, biller_id=<registered_biller_id>, idempotency_key=<same_fixed_key> (first call)
POST /billpay with identical payload and same idempotency_key (second call)
trace: R-FIN-032: payment scheduled; idempotent retry must not double-schedule
STC: PENDING -> SCHEDULED (first call); SCHEDULED -> SCHEDULED (duplicate call, no state change)
tests.executable:
import uuid
import pytest

def test_billpay_idempotency_duplicate_key_returns_same_payment(
    api_client, registered_biller, auth_headers
):
    idem_key = str(uuid.uuid4())
    payload = {
        "biller_id": registered_biller["id"],
        "amount": "200.00",
        "idempotency_key": idem_key,
    }
    resp1 = api_client.post("/billpay", json=payload, headers=auth_headers)
    assert resp1.status_code in (200, 201)
    payment_id_1 = resp1.json()["payment_id"]

    resp2 = api_client.post("/billpay", json=payload, headers=auth_headers)
    assert resp2.status_code in (200, 201)
    payment_id_2 = resp2.json()["payment_id"]

    assert payment_id_1 == payment_id_2
